arguments.name: blazor-preview
arguments.prompt:
The user wants to preview (dry-run) what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App project called "MyBlazorApp" in the directory ./test-output with interactive server rendering enabled. They do NOT want the project actually created yet — just a listing of the files and directory structure that would be produced.

Use the `dotnet new blazor` template (or whichever is the correct Blazor Web App template) with the following parameters:
- Project name: MyBlazorApp
- Output directory: ./test-output
- Interactive render mode: Server

To do a dry run, use `dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity server --dry-run` and report all files that would be generated. Show the full directory tree of what would be created.

Current working directory: /tmp/sv-3b65a40b49b84102a502bc82994bdfdf

data.content:
Here are the results of the dry-run. **No files were actually created** — this is purely a preview.

## Command Used

```
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity server --dry-run
```

## Directory Tree That Would Be Generated

```
test-output/
├── Components/
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor.css
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor.css
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor.js
│   │   ├── ReconnectModal.razor
│   │   ├── ReconnectModal.razor.css
│   │   └── ReconnectModal.razor.js
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Counter.razor
│       ├── Error.razor
│       ├── Home.razor
│       ├── NotFound.razor
│       └── Weather.razor
├── MyBlazorApp.csproj
├── Program.cs
├── Properties/
│   └── launchSettings.json
├── appsettings.json
├── appsettings.Development.json
└── wwwroot/
    ├── app.css
    ├── favicon.png
    └── lib/
        └── bootstrap/
            └── dist/
                ├── css/   (16 files — Bootstrap CSS + maps + RTL variants)
                └── js/    (8 files — Bootstrap JS bundles + maps)
```

## Summary

| Category | Count |
|---|---|
| **Razor components** (`.razor`) | 12 |
| **Component CSS** (`.razor.css`) | 3 |
| **Component JS** (`.razor.js`) | 2 |
| **Project files** (`.csproj`, `Program.cs`) | 2 |
| **Config** (`appsettings.*`, `launchSettings.json`) | 3 |
| **Static assets** (`wwwroot/`) | 25 (app.css, favicon, Bootstrap) |
| **Total files** | **47** |

## Post-Creation Action

If you proceed with actual creation, the template would automatically run `dotnet restore` to pull NuGet packages.

## Key Points About This Template

- **Template**: `blazor` (Blazor Web App)
- **Interactive render mode**: `Server` — the `Counter.razor` and `Weather.razor` pages will use interactive Server rendering via SignalR, while the rest of the app uses static SSR by default.
- The project is a **single-project** setup (no separate `.Client` project since the render mode is server-only).

When you're ready to actually create the project, just run the same command without `--dry-run`:
```bash
dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity server
```

data.content:
Here's the dry-run preview — **nothing was created on disk**.

### Files that would be generated (47 total):

```
test-output/
├── Components/
│   ├── App.razor
│   ├── Routes.razor
│   ├── _Imports.razor
│   ├── Layout/
│   │   ├── MainLayout.razor[.css]
│   │   ├── NavMenu.razor[.css/.js]
│   │   └── ReconnectModal.razor[.css/.js]
│   └── Pages/
│       ├── Counter.razor
│       ├── Error.razor
│       ├── Home.razor
│       ├── NotFound.razor
│       └── Weather.razor
├── MyBlazorApp.csproj
├── Program.cs
├── Properties/launchSettings.json
├── appsettings[.Development].json
└── wwwroot/
    ├── app.css, favicon.png
    └── lib/bootstrap/dist/{css,js}/  (24 Bootstrap files)
```

**Key details:**
- **Single-project** setup (no `.Client` project since render mode is server-only)
- `Counter` and `Weather` use interactive Server rendering via SignalR; the rest uses static SSR
- `dotnet restore` would run automatically after creation

When you're ready, just say the word and I'll create it.
